mechanism_narrative: Persistently low and stable inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, creates a predictable economic environment that supports higher equity valuations by reducing uncertainty about future earnings and discount rates, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show a statistically insignificant positive correlation (p > 0.1) with CPIAUCSL readings below 2.5% over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a deteriorating labor market and broader economic contraction, which typically precedes or coincides with a decline in corporate profits and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in real retail sales (RSAFS) and an increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates a demand-side contraction coupled with restrictive monetary policy, which severely dampens corporate earnings expectations and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month % change < -1.0% AND FEDFUNDS 3-month change > 0.5 percentage points
regime_hint: stagflationary environment or demand shock with tightening
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive or flat in more than 30% of periods where RSAFS declines by more than 1.0% and FEDFUNDS rises by more than 0.5 percentage points simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of confirmed economic expansion, robust growth in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates healthy manufacturing and broader economic activity, translating into positive S&P 500 forward returns as earnings expectations improve.
failure_condition: In periods identified as economic expansion, INDPRO increases of more than 0.5% over 3 months do not lead to significantly positive SPX 5-day returns (t-test p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) sustained above a certain threshold indicates a restrictive monetary policy stance, increasing the cost of capital and reducing corporate profitability, thereby leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 35% of periods when FEDFUNDS is above 3.0% for 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: When non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) are growing strongly but housing starts (HOUST) are declining significantly, it suggests an imbalance where labor market strength is not translating into broader economic activity, possibly due to rising interest rates, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are consistently positive (average return > 0.5%) when PAYEMS is strong and HOUST is weak, contradicting the hypothesized negative impact.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in real Gross Domestic Product (GDPC1) reflects a healthy and expanding economy, which generally supports corporate earnings and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation (p > 0.1) with positive GDPC1 quarterly growth over any 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In an environment of already low unemployment, further declines in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signal an overheating labor market, which often prompts central bank tightening and can lead to negative S&P 500 forward returns as inflation concerns rise.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 4.0% AND UNRATE 3-month change < -0.1 percentage points
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are consistently positive (average return > 0.3%) when UNRATE is below 4.0% and declining, contradicting the hypothesized negative impact.
